prose:
**Ethylene frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO frontier-orbital gap. Level of theory/method used: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly B3LYP/def2-TZVP, run with PySCF (`program`: `pyscf`); density fitting was not requested. Geometry: fixed user-supplied XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz` (6 atoms), not reported as re-optimized by this frontier-orbitals task. Environment: gas phase (`solvent`: null; no implicit or explicit solvent). Charge 0, multiplicity 1. SCF converged: true in 6 cycles. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true. Provenance: computed.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV.
HOMO = -7.6155818 eV; LUMO = -0.17986527 eV. The gap is a Koopmans-style orbital energy gap, not an excitation energy.

Additional returned values: total electronic energy = -78.623486 hartree = -2139.4541 eV; n_orbitals = 86; n_occupied = 8.

Warnings: no warnings were reported in the result JSON (no `warnings` entries were returned by the tool).

Reproducibility: CLI invocation returned by chemkit was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphzrs7r0_/live.json`. The tool result did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully report one beyond the returned JSON output path in the invocation.
